//: Managed-settings snippet to push to a developer's machine: it DECLARES the CrossLoom marketplace (so the dev never runs /plugin marketplace add) and turns on SILENT auto-update. Place in the ClaudeCode managed-settings file (Windows: C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json — the admin must create that directory and file; it is not auto-created). MERGE the extraKnownMarketplaces key if the file already holds other managed settings. See docs/AUTO-UPDATE.md.
//source: Use source=github + repo. It git-CLONES, which is what makes the manifest's relative plugin source (./plugins/crossloom) resolve — the 'url' form downloads ONLY marketplace.json and nothing else, so it can never serve this marketplace, public or private. It needs NO credentials: this marketplace repo is public, and on a machine with no GitHub SSH key Claude Code logs 'SSH not configured, cloning via HTTPS' and clones over plain HTTPS. Verified 2026-07-12 end-to-end with SSH disabled, the git credential helper emptied, and terminal prompts off.
//autoUpdate: NOT the default, and NOT written by 'marketplace add'. Without it the plugin installs once and then never moves again — while continuing to report itself as perfectly healthy. This flag is the whole point of this file.
//identity: Marketplace identity is not normalized. On a machine that already carries a DIFFERENT source form for 'crossloom', this entry will COEXIST with the old one rather than replace it, and the stale one errors at every launch. Replace the existing entry; do not add beside it.
